articles.title: Novelis Korea executive to lead KONMA's new aluminium committee
articles.summary: Choi Min-young, Director of Government Affairs at Novelis Korea, has been appointed inaugural chair of the Aluminium Committee of the Korea Nonferrous Metals Association (KONMA), a newly established body to promote the sustainable development of South Korea's aluminium industry and address shared priorities including trade, environmental regulation and recycled-content policy.

articles.title: Constellium to source local renewable energy for its German facilities
articles.summary: Constellium signed a 10-year power purchase agreement with the community of Gottmadingen to receive the full output of the Katzental solar park for its Gottmadingen and Singen plants in Germany, covering over 25% of combined electricity needs and expected to avoid approximately 3,500 tCO₂/year from spring 2027.
articles.novelis_angle: Constellium's long-term solar PPA at German plants advances its low-carbon credentials; Novelis's Nachterstedt and Göttingen plants will face similar customer and sustainability reporting expectations to demonstrate equivalent renewable-energy sourcing.

articles.title: Macro and fundamentals intertwined with bullish and bearish forces, short-term aluminium prices expected to mainly consolidate and adjust
articles.summary: US June CPI contracted 0.4% month-on-month — the first monthly decline since 2020 — while Federal Reserve chair Warsh signalled zero tolerance for inflation, lifting the September rate-hike probability to 53.5%; combined with weak off-season downstream demand and renewed US airstrikes against Iran on 16 July, LME aluminium is forecast to trade in a USD 3,050–3,250/t range near-term.
